text.heading: MSMSE :Lagos canvasses for change in funding policy
text.p: The Lagos State Government has called for the evolution of a new framework on the funding policy of Micro, Small and Medium Scale Enterprises (MSMSEs) in the country.

Speaking at the commencement of a one-day stakeholders forum on funding model for MSMSE, at Ikeja on Tuesday, the Lagos State Commissioner for Commerce and Industry, Mr. Adeniye Oyemade, said the development of a realistic and sustainable policy framework for funding had become necessary if the sector must grow.

He observed that despite the existing credit schemes for Micro, Small and Medium Scale Enterprises (MSMSE), the problem of easy access to institutional credit was still a problem to the development of the sector.

Oyemade told the gathering which comprises of representatives from the banking sectors and government officials, that lack of such framework had remained unaddressed by the existing policies and legislation.
According to him, "Despite the existing credit schemes which have been dogged with various inadequacies, the problems of easy access to institutional credit still bellies the development of MSMSE.

"I am therefore of the view that this forum would create the opportunity for the evolution of a framework on funding policy for MSMSE, which would serve as the best option for accessibility to institutional financing.

"By organising this form, the state government is taking the lead in effort to foster interaction amongst identified stakeholders by playing the role of motivator and catalyst to determine the direction for formulation of broad objectives that would serve as building blocks fro development of framework."

Delivering the keynote address, the Permanent Secretary to the Ministry of Commerce and Industry, Mr. Wale Raji, noted that such framework should urgently be evolved as the sector has the potential for high job creation.
Raji said full exploitation of the sub-sector’s potential required joint efforts to tackle the problem and proffer solution.
"Poverty has been identified as a critical problem whose eradication can only be achieved through a well thought out, properly articulated and realistic strategies," he said.
The Permanent Secretary said that one of the greatest challenges of policy formulation is its design, followed by sequencing.

"For a government that sincerely wants to overcome these challenges and have policies for implementation that enjoy the buy-in of all stakeholders, both formulators and operators need to work hand-in-hand in order to have meaningful execution of the guidelines in the policy framework," he said.

The forum, which was chaired by the state Commissioner for Establishment and Training, Mr. Jide Sanwoolu, also attracted the Special Adviser to the Governor for Commerce and Industry, Mrs. Olusola Aworu, corporate organisations, representatives of Nigeria Economic Summit Group, among others.
